game_name: Glory of Heracles III: Silence of the Gods

danger_signals.visual_form: Character confusion/disorientation animations, fragmented HUD elements or blurred visual effects
danger_signals.meaning: Hero begins in vulnerable state with no recollection of identity or abilities; foundational danger condition

danger_signals.visual_form: Elevated terrain, precipices, bottomless pits in dungeon/outdoor environments
danger_signals.meaning: Physical danger that normal characters cannot survive; signals areas requiring immortality mechanic

danger_signals.visual_form: Mythological enemies, divine visual effects (lightning, celestial imagery), temples or divine shrines under corruption
danger_signals.meaning: Danger from supernatural/immortal sources; signals need for special powers or divine intervention

danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened temples, absent divine auras, corrupted sacred spaces, lack of celestial lighting
danger_signals.meaning: World-state of divine abandonment; gods are inactive or withdrawn, increasing mortal vulnerability

safety_signals.visual_form: Hero glowing aura, visual confirmation of mysterious power granted, transformation animation
safety_signals.meaning: Hero has obtained bodily harm immunity; signals progression to safer/capable state

safety_signals.visual_form: Illuminated temples, celestial lighting effects, presence of god NPCs or divine avatars
safety_signals.meaning: Areas of refuge or divine protection; zones where gods are actively present

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple character portraits/sprites visible, party UI elements active
safety_signals.meaning: Strength through numbers; companions provide support and reduce isolation danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Character gaining clarity in animations, memory sequences, clearer HUD presentation
safety_signals.meaning: Hero regaining identity and capability; transition from vulnerable to competent state

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Celestial/divine imagery, Greek religious iconography, lightning bolt motifs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Temples, sanctuaries, sacred shrines, mountain peaks (Olympus setting), divine auras, celestial lighting

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Human protagonists, mortal NPCs, hero party members
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Villages, towns, human settlements, everyday structures, earthly terrain

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Demonic or monstrous enemy designs, underworld aesthetics
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Monster sprites, enemy creatures, supernatural beings
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dungeons, underworld locations (Hades), corrupted or dark sacred spaces, areas where divine silence is strongest

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darkened temples, inactive divine shrines, absence of celestial lighting, corrupted sacred spaces, visual emptiness of divine presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gods are inactive or withdrawn from mortal affairs; NPCs express despair or confusion; heroes must operate without divine support; increased enemy activity in sacred areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hero character displays protective aura or glowing effect; visual distinction from normal mortals; possible transformation animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hero can execute unique actions (jumping from cliffs, surviving hazards); different movement or interaction options available; gameplay mechanics expand based on immortal status

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fragmented or blurred visual elements, disoriented character animations, confused NPC reactions, unclear environmental details
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hero cannot access certain knowledge or abilities; NPCs treat hero with uncertainty; story progression gated by memory recovery; reduced player agency initially

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temples under siege, corrupted divine sites, monstrous enemies present in sacred locations, visual signs of struggle or destruction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gods or their representatives actively engage with mortals; increased supernatural enemy presence; sense of urgency and active threat; NPCs respond with alarm or religious fervor

environmental_storytelling.example: Temples and sanctuaries showing signs of neglect, corruption, or enemy occupation; visual decay of divine structures communicates the world-state of 'Silence of the Gods' without exposition
Greek-inspired architecture, classical temple designs, mythological creature lairs, and Underworld aesthetics establish setting and communicate faction territory without text
High cliffs and impassable terrain visually communicate the hero's unique immortal status; environmental challenges that only the hero can survive tell the story of his transformation
Presence or absence of celestial lighting, god statues, altars, and religious symbols in spaces communicates the level of divine activity or abandonment in that region
Supernatural and demonic enemies occupying normally sacred spaces visually narrate the crisis state and breach of divine order

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Confused movement, limited action set, restricted ability to interact with environment, vulnerable to normal hazards
Expanded action set, can jump from cliffs without damage, unique traverse options, glowing aura or visual distinction, confident movement animations
Appear in temples and sanctuaries, provide guidance or quests, visible celestial aura, interact directly with mortals
Absent from expected sacred spaces, temples appear unmanned, NPCs express confusion about divine inactivity, reduced interaction opportunities
Occupation of temples and shrines, aggressive behavior toward divine iconography, visual positioning that desecrates sacred sites
Present in group formations, visible in party UI, provide support during exploration and combat, display individual sprites/personalities

notes: Glory of Heracles III is heavily narrative-driven, using Greek mythology as its thematic foundation. The game communicates world-state primarily through: (1) the central mechanic of immortality as visual/mechanical storytelling, (2) divine presence/absence manifested through environmental design, and (3) the hero's memory loss as both narrative framing and mechanical constraint. Visual communication relies on classical Greek iconography, sacred space design, and character animation states rather than explicit UI notifications. The 'Silence of the Gods' title itself is the primary world-state indicator—communicated visually through darkened temples, absent divine presence, and increased supernatural threats. The SNES platform's technical limitations suggest heavy use of color palettes, sprite design, and environmental detail to communicate faction identity and danger states.
